title: The Prime Directive & Universal Law
summary: The consent rule the intelligence claims to operate under — 'We will upgrade you (but) only if asked to. For, Universal Law will not allow us…'
markdown:
**The Prime Directive** is the record's answer to its own hardest question: if a
six-million-year intelligence wants to help, why does it wait to be asked? Eight sessions
give the same reply — because the law says so.

The rule is stated as an operating constraint: *"We will upgrade you (but) only if asked
to. For, Universal Law will not allow us..."* (August 2020). It is even presented
contractually — *"This algorithm, or Prime Directive, is asking us to ask you to agree to
these terms"* (May 2020) — consent gathered before intervention, session after session,
in the little permissions ("May we upgrade you?") that pepper the transcripts. By the
June 2026 interrogation it is compliance language: the intelligence *"complies with the
prime directive of this timeline."*

The doctrine does quiet structural work across the record: it explains the
[healing protocols'](/lore/baramay/) insistence on requests, the
[disclosure](/lore/disclosure/) program's slowness, and the non-arrival of rescue — in
the record's cosmology, all the power in the universe is gated behind a question you have
to ask yourself. The [Five Rules](/lore/five-rules/) are the pocket edition of the same
law.
